rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 6, 2020 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT KEVIN DORGAN MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to paragraph 27 of the Cross-Complaint. This part of the motion is untimely as to the Cross-Complaint filed on August 18, 2020. As an initial matter regarding he Supplemental Cross-Complaint the Court notes that neither the Cross-Complaint nor the Supplemental Cross-Complaint assert an independent claim for slander of title; however factual allegations stating this claim appear in the Supplemental Cross-Complainant Allegations regarding allegedly improper recordation of the lis pendens form factual basis of the six identified claims in the Cross-Complaint and seek relief in accordance with those claims. Therefore, upon shifting of the burden by the moving party, Cross-Complaint had to show probability of prevailing on the merits on the six claims to the extent they arise out of the recordation of the lis pendens. Thus, the Court required supplemental briefing twice expecting it to address the second prong of ANTI-SLAPP, i.e. to provide argument and evidence as to the probability of prevailing on the merits for the asserted claims of Intentional Interference with Contractual Relations, Elder Abuse, IIED, Fraud, Conspiracy and Constructive Trust to the extent these claims arise out of the recordation of the lis pendens as asserted in the Supplemental Cross-Complaint. Recordation of the lis pendens falls squarely within the protections of ANTI-SLAPP. La Jolla Group II v. Bruce (2012) 211 Cal. App. 4th 461. The only exception to such protection relied upon by the Cross-Complaint is stated in Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal. App. 4th 1275. Cross-Complainant failed to cite any authority or provide any analysis for the proposition that recordation of a non-code compliant lis pendens is "illegal as a matter of law." No cases are cited for this proposition. Moving party has successfully shifted the burden under the first prong of ANTI-SLAPP. As to the second prong, Cross-Complainant correctly argues that recordation of a non-code complaint lis pendens in not privileged under CC 47(b). Palmer v. Zaklama (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1367, 1380. Cross-Complaints burden at this point was to show probability of prevailing on its six claims based on the recordation of lis pendens. Cross-Complainant failed to do so. Thus, any claims arising out of the recordation of lis pendens for Intentional Interference with Contractual Relations, Elder Abuse, IIED, Fraud, Conspiracy and Constructive Trust are stricken. Motion is GRANTED for an additional reason as to the cause of action for Constructive Trust. Cross-Complainant agreed that this claim (arising out of recordation of lis pendens) should be withdrawn. To the extent the supplemental complaint asserts a claim for slander of title arising out of the recordation of the lis pendens, the ANTI-SLAPP is DENIED. Sufficient evidence provided to withstand an ANTI-SLAPP motion on this claim. Any issue with propriety of adding a new cause of action via a supplemental cross-complaint is not a proper subject of the ANTI-SLAPP motion. In short, the Court finds that the moving party shifted its burden re: Supplemental Complaint claims arising out of the recordation of the lis pendens. Plaintiff has shown probability of prevailing on the merits on slander of title claim; Plaintiff failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits as to the Intentional Interference with Contractual Relations, Elder Abuse, IIED, Fraud, Conspiracy and Constructive Trust claims arising out of the recordation of the lis pendens. =(501/CFH)

PLAINTIFF MARIA LUBARSKY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To Complaint is GRANTED as follows: Demurrer will be placed back on calendar to be heard on December 18, 2020.
